title: Systems and methods to control an autonomous mobile robot
abstract: A method for controlling one or more operations of an autonomous mobile robot maneuverable within a home includes establishing wireless communication between an autonomous mobile robot and a remote computing system and, in response to receiving a wireless command signal from the remote computing system, initiating one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot. The autonomous mobile robot is remote from an audio media device stationed within the home. The audio media device is capable of receiving and emitting audio. The remote computing system is configured to associate identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of the audio media device. The wireless command signal corresponds to an audible user command received by the audio media device.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for controlling one or more operations of an autonomous mobile robot maneuverable within a home, the method comprising: establishing wireless communication between an autonomous mobile robot and a remote computing system, the autonomous mobile robot being remote from an audio media device stationed within the home, the audio media device being capable of receiving and emitting audio, and the remote computing system being configured to associate identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of the audio media device; and in response to receiving a wireless command signal from the remote computing system, initiating one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot, the wireless command signal corresponding to an audible user command received by the audio media device. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more operations include autonomously navigating about the home relative to the audio media device. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the autonomous mobile robot comprises an autonomous cleaning robot, and the one or more operations include autonomously navigating to a docking station for the autonomous mobile robot. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more operations include autonomously navigating toward the audio media device. 5. The method of claim 2, further comprising: receiving a position signal from the remote computing system, the position signal being indicative of the location of the audio media device within the home; and autonomously navigating about the home relative to the audio media device while localizing to the location of the audio media device. 6. The method of claim 2, further comprising, while autonomously navigating about the home, generating a map of the home including positions of devices in wireless communication with the remote computing system. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more operations include pausing a cleaning operation of the autonomous mobile robot. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein initiating the one or more operations includes initiating a cleaning operation of the autonomous mobile robot. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: autonomously navigating about the home while performing a cleaning operation; and in response to receiving the wireless command signal from the remote computing system, initiating a localized cleaning operation within a portion of the home by navigating the autonomous mobile robot in a predefined pattern within the portion of the home and increasing an amount of power delivered to a cleaning apparatus of the autonomous mobile robot. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more operations include emitting an audible signal. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more operations include storing a user- defined schedule to perform a subsequent operation at a future time; and performing the subsequent operation at the future time. 12. A method for controlling one or more operations of an autonomous mobile robot maneuverable within a home, the method comprising: associating identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of an audio media device capable of receiving and emitting audio, the audio media device being stationed within the home at a location remote from the autonomous mobile robot; establishing wireless communication with each of the autonomous mobile robot and the audio media device; and 

 transmitting, upon receiving a wireless instruction signal from the audio media device, a wireless command signal to the autonomous mobile robot to initiate one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot, the wireless instruction signal corresponding to an audible user command received by the audio media device. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein: the audible user command includes an audible identifier indicative of the identification data of the autonomous mobile robot and a voice command corresponding to the one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot; the method further comprises generating the wireless command signal based on the voice command and selecting a destination for the wireless command signal based on the audible identifier. 14. The method of claim 12, further comprising: determining, upon receiving an initial user command including an audible identifier indicative of a robot type, that the identification data of the autonomous mobile robot includes the robot type and other identification data of other robots associated with the identification data of the audio media device include the robot type, and then transmitting a wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible request for an audible identifier indicative of a unique identity of the autonomous mobile robot, wherein the audible user command corresponds to a user response to the audible request. 15. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving an initial audible user command including an audible identifier indicative of an identification of another autonomous mobile robot that is not associated with the audio media device; and transmitting a wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible notification indicating that the other autonomous mobile robot is not associated with the audio media device. 16. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving an initial audible user command that does not include any one of a plurality of available voice commands corresponding to the one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot; and transmitting a wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible notification indicating that the initial audible user command does not include a voice command corresponding to any one of the available voice commands. 17. The method of claim 12, further comprising estimating a position of the user based on an acoustic characteristic of the audible user command, wherein transmitting the wireless command signal comprises transmitting the wireless command signal to cause the autonomous mobile robot to navigate toward the estimated position of the user. 18. The method of claim 12, further comprising associating identification data of another autonomous mobile robot with the identification data of the audio media device, the identification data of the autonomous mobile robot corresponding to a first audible identifier and the identification data of the other autonomous mobile robot corresponding to a second audible identifier distinct from the first audible identifier. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein: the audible user command comprises an audible location identifier corresponding to a predefined location within the home, and the method further comprises: associating identification data of another autonomous mobile robot with the identification data of the audio media device; and transmitting the wireless command signal to the autonomous mobile robot comprises transmitting the wireless command signal to the autonomous mobile robot upon determining that a distance of the autonomous mobile robot to the predefined location is less than a distance of the other autonomous mobile robot to the predefined location. 20. The method of claim 12, wherein the audible user command comprises an audible location identifier corresponding to one predefined location of a plurality of predefined locations 

 within the home, and transmitting the wireless command signal to the autonomous mobile robot comprises transmitting the wireless command signal to the autonomous mobile robot to cause the autonomous mobile robot to navigate to the one predefined location. 21. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving an initial audible user command including an audible scheduling parameter corresponding to a future time for transmitting the wireless command signal to the autonomous robot to perform the one or more operations, and transmitting, upon determining that the future time conflicts with an event in a user event calendar, a wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible notification indicating the future time conflicts with the event. 22. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving an initial audible user command to schedule an event in a user event calendar, transmitting, upon determining that the event corresponds to a predetermined event type, a wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible request, wherein the audible user command includes confirmation of the audible request, and transmitting the wireless command signal comprises transmitting the wireless command signal to the autonomous mobile robot to initiate one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot within a predefined period before the event. 23. A method of providing status of an autonomous mobile robot maneuverable within a home, the method comprising: establishing wireless communication between an audio media device and a remote computing system, the audio media device being capable of receiving and emitting audio and being stationed within a home at a location remote from the autonomous mobile robot, and the remote computing system being configured to associate identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of the audio media device; and emitting an audible signal that represents an operating status of the autonomous mobile robot in response to receiving a wireless notification signal from the remote computing system, the wireless notification signal corresponding to status data received by the remote computing 

 system as the autonomous mobile robot autonomously navigates about the home relative to the audio media device. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal comprises emitting the audible signal after determining that a user has entered the home. 25. The method of claim 23, wherein the operating status of the autonomous mobile robot includes a schedule of previous operations, and emitting the audible signal includes emitting an audible request to modify the stored schedule of operations based on the schedule of previous operations. 26. The method of claim 23, wherein the operating status corresponds to a stasis condition of the autonomous mobile robot within the home, and emitting the audible signal comprises emitting the audible signal to indicate that the autonomous mobile robot is in the stasis condition. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the audible signal indicates a location of the autonomous mobile robot within the home. 28. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal includes emitting the audible signal to indicate a frequency of previous operations of the autonomous mobile robot. 29. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal includes emitting the audible signal to indicate a total duration of previous operations of the autonomous mobile robot within a predefined period. 30. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal includes emitting the audible signal to indicate an estimated remaining service life of a component of the autonomous mobile robot. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the component of the autonomous mobile robot is a cleaning brush, a cleaning pad, a roller, a battery, debris bin, or a wheel module. 32. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal includes emitting the audible signal to identify an obstacle detected by the autonomous mobile robot while the autonomous mobile robot navigates within the home. 33. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal includes emitting the audible signal to identify a connected device located within the home, the connected device being wirelessly connected to the remote computing system and being associated with the audio media device. 34. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal comprises emitting audible instructions to guide a user to address a basis of an error associated with the autonomous mobile robot in response to detection of the error. 35. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal includes emitting the audible signal after the autonomous mobile robot autonomously navigates about the home, the audible signal identifying an area of the home that the autonomous mobile robot did not traverse while autonomously navigating. 36. The method of claim 23, wherein emitting the audible signal comprises emitting the audible signal upon receiving an audible user request for the operation status of the autonomous mobile robot. 37. A method of providing status of an autonomous mobile robot positioned within a home, the method comprising: associating identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of an audio media device capable of receiving and emitting audio, the audio media device being stationed within the home at a location remote from the autonomous mobile robot; 

 establishing wireless communication with each of the autonomous mobile robot and the audio media device; and upon receiving data indicative of an operating status of the autonomous mobile robot, transmitting a wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible signal that represents the operating status of the autonomous mobile robot. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein: transmitting the wireless notification signal comprises, upon determining that an estimating remaining service life of a replaceable component of the autonomous mobile robot is less than a predefined threshold, transmitting the wireless notification signal to cause the audio media device to emit the audible signal, the audible signal including an offer to purchase a replacement component for the component of the autonomous mobile robot, and the method further comprises placing an order for the replacement component upon receiving an audible user command including a confirmation to purchase the replacement component. 39. The method of claim 37, wherein associating the identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with the identification data of the audio media device comprises: transmitting a wireless command signal based on emission and receipt of an audible association request, wherein one of the autonomous mobile robot and the audio media device emits the audible association request and the other of the autonomous mobile robot and the audio media device receives the audible association request; and associating the identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with the identification data of the audio media device upon receiving confirmation of the receipt of the audible associated request. 40. The method of claim 37, further comprising determining a location of the autonomous mobile robot within the home based on an audible status signal emitted by the autonomous mobile robot and received by the audio media device, wherein emitting the audible signal comprises emitting the audible signal to indicate the location of the autonomous mobile robot. 41. A method for controlling one or more operations of an autonomous mobile robot maneuverable within a home, and the method comprising: establishing wireless communication between an audio media device and a remote computing system, the audio media device being capable of receiving and emitting audio and being stationed within a home at a location remote from the autonomous mobile robot, and the remote computing system being configured to associate identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of the audio media device; and in response to receiving an audible user command, transmitting a wireless instruction signal to the remote computing system to initiate one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot. 42. A method of providing status of an autonomous mobile robot maneuverable within a home, the method comprising: establishing wireless communication between an autonomous mobile robot and a remote computing system, the autonomous mobile robot being remote from an audio media device stationed within the home, the audio media device being capable of receiving and emitting audio, and the remote computing system being configured to associate identification data of the autonomous mobile robot with identification data of the audio media device; and while autonomously navigating about the home relative to the audio media device, transmitting, from the autonomous mobile robot to the remote computing system, status data indicative of an operating status of the autonomous mobile robot to cause the audio media device to emit an audible signal that represents the operating status of the autonomous mobile robot. 43. An autonomous mobile robot comprising a chassis; a sensing system to generate signals for generating a map of a home; locomotive members to support the chassis on a floor surface within the home, the locomotive members being drivable to autonomously navigate the autonomous mobile robot about the floor surface while the sensing system generates the signals for generating the map of the home; 

 a controller operable with a wireless communication system, the controller being configured to initiate, in response to receiving a wireless command signal from a remote computing system, one or more operations of the autonomous mobile robot, the wireless command signal corresponding to an audible user command received by an audio media device capable of receiving and emitting audio. 44. The autonomous mobile robot of claim 43, wherein the mobile robot is remote from the audio media device, and the audio media device is stationed within the home, and wherein the controller is further configured to establish wireless communication with the remote computing system when the remote computing system associates identification data of the mobile robot with identification data of the audio media device. 45. The autonomous mobile robot of claim 43, further comprising the audio media device, the audio media device being mounted to the chassis of the autonomous mobile robot. 46. The autonomous mobile robot of claim 43, further comprising: a variable height member on the chassis, the variable height member being configured to move vertically relative to the chassis to a plurality of camera height positions; and a camera supported by the variable height member. 47. The autonomous mobile robot of claim 46, wherein the sensing system comprises the camera, and the camera is configured to capture images of the home for generating the map of the home. 48. The autonomous mobile robot of claim 46, wherein the one or more operations initiated by the controller comprise autonomously navigating the autonomous mobile robot to a selected location within the home and moving the variable height member to a selected camera height position to observe, using the camera, an object within the home, the object being identified in the audible user command received by the audio media device. 49. An autonomous mobile robot comprising: 

 at least one drive wheel operable to move the mobile robot across a floor surface; a sensor system including a navigation sensor; a controller in communication with the sensor system, the controller configured to control movement of the mobile robot based on sensor signals generated by the navigation sensor; and a wireless communication system in communication with the controller, wherein the controller is configured to initiate, in response to receiving a wireless command signal from a remote computing system, one or more operations of the mobile robot, the wireless command signal being indicative of a user requested action and being based on an audible user command received by an audio media device capable of receiving and emitting audio. 50. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the audio media device is in wireless communication with the remote computing system and stationed within an environment at a location remote from the autonomous mobile robot. 51. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the controller is configured to generate a map of an environment based on the sensor signals. 52. The mobile robot of claim 51, wherein the navigation sensor comprises a camera configured for visual identification of features and landmarks used in calculating a robot pose on the generated map. 53. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the mobile robot is configured to perform multiple missions, and transmit a wireless signal indicative of cumulative data measured by the sensor system for each of the multiple missions to cause the audio media device to emit a cumulative summary based on the cumulative data. 54. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein: the sensor system is configured to provide data indicative of a robot status to the controller, and 

 the mobile robot is configured to transmit a wireless signal indicative of the robot status to cause the audio media device to emit an audible status update. 55. The mobile robot of claim 54, wherein the data is indicative of a total duration that the mobile robot has been operating across multiple missions, a number of missions that the mobile robot has performed, a total cumulative distance of traversed floor surface, or a distance that the mobile robot has travelled. 56. The mobile robot of claim 54, wherein: the mobile robot is a vacuum cleaning robot, and the sensor system is configured to provide data indicative of a total amount of debris collected by the mobile robot across multiple missions. 57. The mobile robot of claim 54, wherein: the mobile robot is a vacuum cleaning robot operable with a docking station that evacuates debris from the vacuum cleaning robot, and the mobile robot is configured to send, to the remote computing system via the wireless communication system, data to enable the remote computing system to calculate an average number of evacuation operations per week, a number of evacuation operations performed during a certain period of time, or a number of evacuation operations performed over an area covered by the mobile robot during a certain period of time. 58. The mobile robot of claim 49, further comprising: a serviceable component, and one or more sensors configured to generate signals indicative of an estimated service life of the serviceable component. 59. The mobile robot of claim 58, wherein the serviceable component comprises a cleaning brush, a cleaning pad, a roller, a blade, a battery, a bin, a wheel, a bin filter, or a bin lid. 60. The mobile robot of claim 59, wherein the controller is configured to transmit a wireless signal indicative of an estimated remaining service life of the serviceable component in response to an audible user command received by the audio media device. 61. The mobile robot of claim 49, further comprising a stall sensor unit configured to detect a stalled state of the mobile robot, wherein the controller is configured to transmit a wireless signal indicative of the detected stalled state of the mobile robot to cause the audio media device to emit a status update indicating the mobile robot is in the stalled state. 62. The mobile robot of claim 61, wherein the stall sensor unit includes an optical stasis sensor, a motor stall sensor, a mouse sensor, a gyroscope, an accelerometer, or stereo cameras. 63. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the controller is configured to transmit, in response to the sensor system detecting an error, a wireless signal indicative of the error to cause the audio media device to emit an initial audible instruction to address the error. 64. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein: the mobile robot is a vacuum cleaning robot, and the controller is configured to control movement of the vacuum cleaning robot to a user- specified room in an environment in response to receiving the wireless command signal, wherein the audible user command and the wireless command signal are indicative of the user-specified room. 65. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein: the mobile robot is a vacuum cleaning robot, and the controller is configured to adjust a vacuum strength in response to the wireless command signal. 66. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the controller is configured to adjust a stored schedule of operations in response to the wireless command signal. 67. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the mobile robot is configured to transmit, in response to the wireless command signal, a wireless signal to cause the audio media device to emit an audible signal indicative of a current location of the mobile robot. 68. The mobile robot of claim 49, wherein the mobile robot is a vacuum cleaning robot comprising: a debris bin; rotatable rollers configured to agitate debris from the floor surface; and an air mover configured to move the debris from the floor surface toward the debris bin. 69. The mobile robot of claim 68, further comprising a debris bin level sensor configured to detect an amount of debris ingested into the debris bin. 70. The mobile robot of claim 68, further comprising one or more debris sensors configured to detect a rate of debris ingestion. 71. The mobile robot of claim 68, further comprising a debris filter and a filter sensor to detect whether the debris filter requires cleaning.
